payload.title: Snapshot inspection still burns operator context
payload.summary: While repairing Bob work visibility, a narrow rg over task-lifecycle-snapshot.json and related artifacts still produced a very large transcript dump. This repeated the same class of context-burn failure that local evidence pipeline work was meant to prevent.
evidence: Command output from rg over .ai/task-lifecycle-snapshot.json expanded into hundreds of lines despite a bounded diagnostic intent.
The useful facts were small: stale andrey-user.Kevin existed in agent_roster, task 59 was assigned to Bob, and the snapshot needed regeneration.
proposal: Add compact snapshot-inspection helpers or default guards so task lifecycle snapshots and large exported evidence files are summarized by finding/count/path unless explicitly opened raw.
Teach architect-loop tooling to avoid direct rg/cat over known bulky generated artifacts and route such reads through local evidence/extraction helpers.
payload.recommendation: Treat as ergonomics/CAPA follow-up for evidence-pipeline adoption and command-output boundedness.
